steps.title: Find the y-intercept by setting x = 0
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is where the graph crosses the $y$-axis, which occurs at $x = 0$. Substitute this value into the given equation.

steps.title: Substitute and evaluate
steps.content:
$$y = -(4)^0 - 39$$ Since $(4)^0 = 1$: $$y = -(1) - 39 = -40$$
The $y$-intercept is the point $(0, -40)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
